properties.Title: COTA's Sustainable Non-Timber Forest Products (NTFP) Project
properties.Description: Initiated in 2019 by the Cree Outfitting and Tourism Association (COTA), the overall project aims to improve the quality of the visitor experience in Eeyou Istchee by integrating non-timber forest products (NTFPs), mainly edible mushrooms and berries, into local restaurants and tourism offerings. A study was conducted in collaboration with FaunENord along the Billly Diamond Highway and along some of the access roads and around Waswanipi and Mistissini to gain a better understanding of the distribution and abundance of NTFPs. In the summer 2024, an inventory was also done on several islands near the communities of Waskaganish, Eastmain, Wemindji and Chisasibi.
properties.Contact:
Rob Imrie  Director of Development
Robin McGinley Executive Director

Emmanuelle Pelletier

properties.Title: ᒋᓵᓰᐲ ᓂᓈᑯ ᐐᒌᐙᑭᒥᐦᒃ Multi-Service Day Centres (MSDCs)
properties.Description: Nanaahkuu Wiichihiiweukamikw / Multi-Service Day Centre (MSDC) is a place for people to gather, to heal, and to learn. The programs are tailored to individuals experiencing social isolation, elders, adults with special needs, and individuals with mental health challenges. Some MSDCs also offer programming for youth. MSDC provides nutritious meals both in-house and through the “meals on wheels” service. They offer a variety of workshops and programs for clients, such as psycho-education and nutritional councelling.
properties.Address: 8 Cow-Boy Trail Street, Waskaganish, QC, Canada, J0M 1R0
properties.Links: MSDC website with contact information for local centres

properties.Title: ᒥᔫᐊᔑᒥᔓᐦ Miyuu-Ashimishuh
properties.Description: The Miyû-Ashimishuh program, offers weekly coupons to pregnant mothers for healthy food until two months post-delivery. Participants can get weekly grocery coupons for nutritious foods such as milk, eggs, fruits and vegetables.  All pregnant woman in Eeyou Istchee can access Miyuu-Ashimishuh, from the 12th week of their pregnancy, up until 8 weeks post-partum.
properties.Contact: Contact your local Awash nurse, PCCR, or nutritionist.

properties.Title:
Training Program on Food Handling Best Practices for Cree
Traditional and Store-bought Foods
properties.Description: The Cree program on the Safe Food Handling workshops given in Inland and Coastal communities every year since 2008 and has resulted in over 150 trained participants. The program has been temporarily interupted for the past year. Training on the skills and practices to ensure that all Cree traditional foods are of the best quality when served at community feasts, family gatherings and institutional settings like the Chisasibi hospital, MSDC’s, Group Homes, Reception Center, Childcare centers and Elder Homes.

properties.Title: Eeyou Ituun Training
properties.Description: The Cree Land Stewardship (Eeyou/Eenou Ituun) Training Program is a fully accredited 900-hours collegial program that leads to an official Attestation of Collegial Studies (ACS) certified by the Québec Ministry of Education. The program is offered in partnership between the Cree Trappers Association and the Cégep de St-Félicien. The Eeyou ltuun, Cree land Stewardship Program is designed to teach Cree who are not experienced land users the fundamental skills to be effective Cree Land Stewards.

Eeyou Eenou Ituun Coordinator/Communication Officer,

properties.Title: Annie Whiskeychan Day
properties.Description: Annie Whiskeychan was an important champion of Cree education. Every year in May or June, the Cree School board honours her contributions with a day of cultural games, activities, and competitions at the local level, and regionally. Many skills developped are relevant to food security such as goose cooking on open fire, goose calling, tea and bannock.

properties.Title: Breakfast Program
properties.Description: Students at the Cree School Board are offered a breakfast through a partnership with local and regional entities, the Cree Nation Government, the Cree Health Board, and Breakfast Club of Canada. This program is offered in highschools and elementary schools in Eeyou Istchee. Prior to the start of classes, youth are provided with cereal bars, yogurt and/or fruit for breakfast.

properties.Title: ᒋᓵᓰᐲ ᓂᓈᑯ ᐐᒌᐙᑭᒥᐦᒃ Multi-Service Day Centres (MSDCs)
properties.Description: Nanaahkuu Wiichihiiweukamikw / Multi-Service Day Centre (MSDC) is a place for people to gather, to heal, and to learn. The programs are tailored to individuals experiencing social isolation, elders, adults with special needs, and individuals with mental health challenges. Some MSDCs also offer programming for youth. MSDC provides nutritious meals both in-house and through the “meals on wheels” service. They offer a variety of workshops and programs for clients, such as psycho-education and nutritional councelling.
properties.Address: 27 Gilpin Road, Wemindji, QC, Canada, J0M 1L0
properties.Links: MSDC website with contact information for local centres

properties.Title: Traditional Food in Cree Health Board Institutions
properties.Description: Traditional food is served in 21 Cree Health Board points of service (elders home, multi-service day centres or MSDCs) in Eeyou Istchee and in the hospital in Chisasibi and Montreal. Food is donated by Cree Hunters trained with a MAPAQ food handlers certificate training and then additional training focusing on traditional food.
properties.Contact:
Lilian Kandiliotis PDt
Chantal Vinet-Lanouette
Public Health Nutritionists
properties.Address: 60 Maquatau Rd Wemindji, QC, Canada, J0M 1L0

properties.Title: ᒥᔫᐊᔑᒥᔓᐦ Miyuu-Ashimishuh
properties.Description: The Miyû-Ashimishuh program, offers weekly coupons to pregnant mothers for healthy food until two months post-delivery. Participants can get weekly grocery coupons for nutritious foods such as milk, eggs, fruits and vegetables.  All pregnant woman in Eeyou Istchee can access Miyuu-Ashimishuh, from the 12th week of their pregnancy, up until 8 weeks post-partum.
properties.Contact: Contact your local Awash nurse, PCCR, or nutritionist.
properties.Address: 60 Maquatau Rd Wemindji, QC, Canada, J0M 1L0

properties.Title: CERRI geodesic dome
properties.Description: The 33-foot diameter geodesic dome greenhouse built in 2023 enhances agricultural capabilities, supports CERRI's mission of food sovereignty, and serves as a model for sustainable building practices.
